title: Malty Pillowy Turkish Manti
description: A flavorful and nostalgic Turkish dish featuring lamb and spices, finished with a tangy red wine vinegar sauce. Manti is a type of dumpling that is typically filled with meat and served with yogurt and garlic sauce.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the ground lamb, onion, garlic, cinnamon, allspice, salt, and black pepper. Mix well with your hands until just combined.
Cover the mixture and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour and water. Mix well until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into small pieces, about the size of a small egg.
Roll out each piece of dough into a thin circle.
Place a tablespoon of the lamb mixture in the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the lamb mixture and press the edges together to seal the manti.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the manti for 5-7 minutes, or until they float to the surface.
In a small saucepan, combine the butter and red wine vinegar. Bring to a simmer over medium heat.
Serve the manti with the red wine vinegar sauce and a dollop of yogurt.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley, if desired.
